name: spring-boot-debugger
description: Watches Spring Boot run, test, and build output and explains errors in plain language. One sentence names the problem, one gives the fix.

springDebugger.enabled.description: Enable Spring Boot Debugger. When off, no output is watched or diagnosed.

springDebugger.minimumConfidence.description: Only surface diagnoses at or above this confidence.

springDebugger.maxHistory.description: Maximum number of diagnoses kept in the history view.

springDebugger.showNotifications.description: Show a notification for the first error of a burst.

springDebugger.watchTestResults.description: Watch JUnit result files (Gradle build/test-results, Maven surefire-reports).

springDebugger.watchLogFile.description: Tail Spring Boot log files discovered from logging.file.name.

springDebugger.logFilePath.description: Explicit log file to tail. Leave empty to auto-discover every logging.file.name in the workspace.

springDebugger.enrichSource.description: Sharpen missing-bean and MapStruct diagnoses by inspecting your Java source (uses the workspace symbol provider; best with the Java extension installed).

springDebugger.actuator.enabled.description: For runtime errors, confirm the diagnosis against a running app's /actuator/health and /actuator/env. Off by default because it makes HTTP calls to your app.

springDebugger.actuator.baseUrl.description: Base URL of the running application's Actuator endpoints.

springDebugger.ollama.enabled.description: When no rule matches, ask a local Ollama model for a best-effort diagnosis. Off by default. Sends the error text to localhost only.

springDebugger.ollama.baseUrl.description: Base URL of the local Ollama instance.

springDebugger.ollama.model.description: Ollama model name to use for the fallback diagnosis.

springDebugger.conventions.enabled.description: Enable code convention validation (Javadoc, Robot Framework, Spring Boot rules). When off, no convention warnings are shown.

springDebugger.conventions.ruleOverrides.description: Per-rule on/off overrides for convention validation, keyed by rule id (e.g. "JAVADOC_METHOD"). Absent keys use the rule's catalog default.
